text: other planets have methane (and probably other  hydrocarbons) with no plants or animals.

replies.author: @adsi6201
replies.text: @oscarvogel2140 methane is a very simple hydrocarbon

replies.author: @oscarvogel2140
replies.text: @adsi6201 Not sure what your point is.

replies.author: @adsi6201
replies.text: @oscarvogel2140 point is that simple organic compounds can exist without life

replies.author: @oscarvogel2140
replies.text: @adsi6201 Oh, so we agree it doesn't take plants/animals to make methane. How about petroleum? Can methane convert to petroleum under pressure and heat?

replies.author: @adsi6201
replies.text: @oscarvogel2140  no, methane cannot turn into petroleum. Petroleum is a way more complex hydrocarbon than methane. It is full of nitrates and benzene rings, which are organic. If petroleum were made from rock it would be full of silicon and boron (borazine). Methane is just CH4

author: @allantulli5546
text: This is not true at all, oil is formed by methyl hydrates and co2 being compressed at the bottom of the ocean and getting pushed into tectonic zones where the heat cooks it into long chain hydrocarbons and it flows along the earths crust. It's a continuous process and we are never going to run out of oil. BS about fossil fuels.

text: Pure assumption, first we’re told fossil fuels which is a lie. The earth creates oil, we’re not running out, just like primary water

author: @TheBlackCatTrader
text: A chemical analysis of crude oil finds no biological  or organic material.

replies.author: @ET_AYY_LMAO
replies.text:
Thats a laughable statement. Crude oil primarily consists of organic compounds, saying it doesn't contain organic material is laughable from a chemistry point of view. Most if not all crude oil has biomarkers such as porphyrins which indicates presence of previously plant based material, as well as molecules that are very likely to be derived from Cholesterol and fatty acids.

Now I like to keep an open mind and if you ask me it wouldnt be unlikely if there existed abiotic oil, but there is generally no doubt that most of the oil we get is not abiotic.

replies.author: @jswets5007
replies.text: It is the fatty oils from algae.

replies.author: @TheBlackCatTrader
replies.text: ​@ET_AYY_LMAO  it has already been proven, with radio spectrometry, that there is absolutely no organic material in crude oil.

replies.author: @stpsupply
replies.text: It's a continuous process that's happened and continues to happen over time... plant and animal life died over and over again across eons and continues to die off to this day.... it gets replenished constantly... today's dead material will be oil in another eon... tomorrow's dead material will be oil an eon plus a day away.... it's similar to the way a compost heap works.... there's plenty of oil and we could all have fuel for less than $1 a gallon but it's all price controlled for maximum profit... like the diamond industry... there's plenty of diamonds as well but it's a heavily controlled industry... they use the fear term of oil being "fossil fuels" to make it sound rare or that it's going to run out because it's made from dinosaur mush and cephalopods and giant ferns... but it's not... they don't find fossils near deep level oil... only in the tar pits and near the surface

replies.author: @AnonymousAnarchist2
replies.text:
There probably is some. But we have not yet found any significant sources of abiotic oil, and the sources suspected of being abiotic also have evidince of microbal life. And that males sense microbes are hardly ones to give up a free meal!
So it seems the concensus is that there probably is some but it would be difficult to impossible to really tell.

And part of that comes down to the conditions required to store oil in earths crust. there are only so many possible oil fields and not all of then would have stored oil. 
But all of them would have been exposed to natural biotic sources in thier formation.

By the way, did you know the estimate for the maximum oil stored in earths crust puts us running out approx in 2060?

And that some possible stores are already known to be less then the etimated maximum?

And for some reason thats utterly baffling and stupifying oil companies keep refusing to accept grant money to switch production over to synthetic and plant based crude. Seriously if you know anyone invested in oil let them know to divest because this is becoming a parody of short sighted politics

replies.author: @naterbator
replies.text: ​​@AnonymousAnarchist2 the reservoirs refill naturally.  Even if crude oil were to run out (it never will)  the amount of natural gas available is  ridiculously absurd.  Around 139 billion cubic meters of natural gas burned off at oil fields each year...they burn natural gas just to get rid of it there's so much of it. 😅

replies.author: @AnonymousAnarchist2
replies.text:
@jswets5007 We have found every possible reserve of crude oil, that we can possibly reach. We have drilled for reserves of crude everywhere possible, we have surveyed all possible locations.
Every one. 
And we did, in fact use up all of the fuel crude oils in the USA. Before 2000, we did not find some new source. We make synthetic fuels from fossil resources of carbon, we do not make fuels from crude oil in the USA. And if that sounds expensive thats because it is. Quite a bit more expesnive then making synthetic fuels from woods and aggie waste. But those dont have big goverment grants backing them because folks like you drink the cool-aid and vote against your wallet and for fossil fuels.

Crude oil takes millions of years to develop we burn through millions of years worth of natural crude oil production yearly, not all crude is useful for fuels, and crude takes very specific enviromental factors that are rare in earths history, and not likely to occur again.

I bloody better know about Crude. I still visit the old family mansion built on crude oil money. It is all but gone, that is reality. Crude oil will be totally gone in 30 years. Period. No ifs, no buts, no exceptions. We have found all the shale, all the oil, all the sources that it is physically possible for there to even be any amount of crude there is at most 30 years left.
And at minimum 5 years left.

Choose wisely
